examples.title: CSMA/CD Algorithm
examples.description: Simplified pseudocode showing collision detection and backoff in Ethernet
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CSMA_CD
BEGIN
  WHILE TRUE DO
    WAIT for frame to transmit
    
    // Carrier Sense
    WHILE medium is busy DO
      WAIT until medium is idle
    END WHILE
    
    // Start Transmission
    START transmitting frame
    
    // Collision Detection
    WHILE transmitting AND no collision DO
      // Listen while talk
      IF collision detected THEN
        STOP transmitting
        SEND jam signal
        WAIT random backoff time
        RETRY transmission
      END IF
    END WHILE
    
    IF no collision THEN
      MARK frame as successfully transmitted
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: CSMA/CD ensures orderly access to shared network medium. Stations listen before transmitting, detect collisions during transmission, send jam signal to notify all stations, and wait random backoff time before retrying.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of retransmission attempts

examples.title: Ethernet Frame Parser
examples.description: Python code to parse and display Ethernet frame fields
examples.code:
import struct

def parse_ethernet_frame(raw_data):
    """Parse Ethernet frame and extract fields"""
    
    # Unpack first 14 bytes (Dest MAC + Src MAC + Type)
    dest_mac, src_mac, ether_type = struct.unpack('!6s6sH', raw_data[:14])
    
    # Convert MAC addresses to readable format
    dest_mac = ':'.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in dest_mac)
    src_mac = ':'.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in src_mac)
    
    # EtherType mapping
    ether_types = {
        0x0800: 'IPv4',
        0x0806: 'ARP',
        0x86DD: 'IPv6',
        0x8100: 'VLAN Tagged'
    }
    
    # Get payload (everything after header)
    payload = raw_data[14:]
    
    # Calculate FCS (CRC32)
    received_fcs = struct.unpack('!I', raw_data[-4:])[0]
    calculated_fcs = crc32(raw_data[:-4]) & 0xFFFFFFFF
    
    frame_info = {
        'Destination MAC': dest_mac,
        'Source MAC': src_mac,
        'EtherType': ether_types.get(ether_type, hex(ether_type)),
        'Payload Length': len(payload),
        'FCS Valid': received_fcs == calculated_fcs
    }
    
    return frame_info

# Example usage
raw_frame = b'\x00\x1a\x2b\x3c\x4d\x5e' + b'\x11\x22\x33\x44\x55\x66' + 
            b'\x08\x00' + b'\x45\x00\x00\x1c' + b'\x00\x00' +
            b'\x40\x00\x01\x11' + b'\x00\x00' + b'\x00\x00' + 
            b'\xc0\xa8\x01\x01' + b'\xc0\xa8\x01\x02'
            
print(parse_ethernet_frame(raw_frame))
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how to parse Ethernet frames, extracting MAC addresses, EtherType, and validating the Frame Check Sequence (FCS) using CRC32.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is frame size

examples.title: Switch MAC Address Learning
examples.description: C code demonstrating MAC address table learning in an Ethernet switch
examples.explanation: This C code simulates an Ethernet switch's MAC address learning mechanism. When a frame arrives, the switch learns the source MAC address and associates it with the incoming port. For forwarding, it looks up the destination MAC; if found, it forwards to that specific port; if unknown, it floods the frame.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m) where m is MAC table size (linear search)
